questions.question: Consider the following statements: • The novel The Blue Umbrella was written by Ruskin Bond. • The book The White Tiger was authored by Aravind Adiga. • The Room on the Roof was written by R. K. Narayan. • The Jungle Book (1894) was written by Rudyard Kipling. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is Correct – The Blue Umbrella → Ruskin Bond Statement 2 is Correct – The White Tiger → Aravind Adiga Statement 3 is Incorrect – The Room on the Roof was written by Ruskin Bond, not R. K. Narayan. Statement 4 is Correct – The Jungle Book → Rudyard Kipling

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the term “brace” in football: • A brace refers to a player scoring two goals in a single match. • A brace means receiving two yellow cards in a match. • The term brace is also used in cricket for taking two wickets in two consecutive balls. • In football, scoring three goals in a match is called a brace. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 – Correct In football, a brace means a player scores two goals in the same match. Example: If a striker scores two goals in one game, commentators say, “He scored a brace.” Statement 2 – Incorrect Two yellow cards lead to a red card (sending off), but this is not called a brace. Statement 3 – Incorrect In cricket, taking two wickets in two consecutive balls is called a brace? No. The correct term is a double strike (informally), but officially there is no term “brace” used in cricket like this. Statement 4 – Incorrect Scoring three goals in football is called a hat-trick, not a brace. Quick Sports Terms 2 goals → Brace 3 goals → Hat-trick 4 goals → Haul (sometimes called “four-goal haul”)

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the growth of tendrils in plants: • Tendrils show directional growth in response to touch. • Uneven growth on opposite sides of a tendril causes it to coil around a support. • This phenomenon is known as phototropism. • Due to uneven growth, the tendril circles around the object and clings to it. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 - Correct Tendrils respond to touch. This type of movement is called thigmotropism (growth movement due to touch stimulus). Statement 2 - Correct When a tendril touches an object, cells on the opposite side grow faster than the touched side. This uneven growth causes the tendril to bend toward the object. Statement 3 - Incorrect The correct term is thigmotropism, not phototropism. Phototropism → response to light Thigmotropism → response to touch Statement 4 - Correct Because of uneven growth, the tendril circles around the object and clings to it, helping the plant to climb.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: • The Human Development Index (HDI) is based on life expectancy and standard of living only. • Life expectancy at birth is taken into account to measure HDI. • Multi-Dimensional Poverty Index (MPI) measures health and education only. • Child mortality and nutrition are taken into account to measure health in MPI. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 - Incorrect HDI is not based only on life expectancy and standard of living. HDI (released by the United Nations Development Programme) is based on three dimensions: • Health → Life expectancy at birth • Education → Mean years of schooling & Expected years of schooling • Standard of living → GNI per capita So, education is missing in Statement 1 → Incorrect. Statement 2 - Correct Life expectancy at birth is one of the core indicators of HDI under the health dimension. Statement 3 - Incorrect MPI does not measure only health and education. MPI measures three dimensions: • Health • Education • Standard of living So, the statement is incomplete → Incorrect. Statement 4 - Correct Under the health dimension of MPI, the indicators include: Nutrition Child mortality So, this statement is correct.

questions.question: When was the East Asia Summit process initiated?
questions.explanation: The East Asia Summit (EAS) process was initiated in 2003 during the ASEAN Summit process. However, the first East Asia Summit was actually held in 2005 in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.

questions.question: As part of Mission Mausam, India is building a cloud chamber at which institute?
options: Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology, Pune
questions.explanation: Under Mission Mausam, the Government of India is establishing a first-of-its-kind convective cloud chamber to improve cloud physics research and aid weather modification studies. This cloud chamber facility is being set up at the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology in Pune. The purpose of this chamber is to simulate the formation and behaviour of clouds under controlled conditions, allowing scientists to better understand cloud processes, rain droplet and ice particle formation, and interactions relevant to Indian monsoon weather

questions.question: How did the Bombay and Madras governments respond to the proposal of the 1839 Draft Act on slavery?
options: Both supported the Act without changes
Bombay supported it, Madras rejected it
Bombay saw no need for a law; Madras questioned its expediency
Both governments demanded full enforcement of the Act
questions.explanation: During discussions on the 1839 Draft Act on slavery: The Bombay Government expressed the view that there was no pressing necessity for a specific anti-slavery law. They believed the issue did not require fresh legislative intervention. The Madras Government did not outright reject the proposal but questioned the expediency (practical usefulness and timing) of introducing such legislation. So, neither presidency strongly supported strict enforcement. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: A – incorrect, both did not support the Act without changes. B – incorrect, Bombay did not clearly support it; Madras did not fully reject it either. D – incorrect, they did not demand strict or full enforcement.

questions.question: Which economist criticized the Second Five-Year Plan for undermining democracy.
questions.explanation: The Second Five-Year Plan (1956–1961) was based on the Mahalanobis Model, which emphasized heavy industries and a large role for the public sector. B. R. Shenoy was the only member of the Planning Commission who dissented from the Plan. He argued that excessive state control, deficit financing, and centralised planning could harm economic freedom and democratic principles. He favoured a market-oriented approach instead of heavy state intervention.

questions.question: Match the government schemes with their correct objectives. Government scheme Objectives A. Dak Karmayogi i. Infrastructure monitoring B. India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) ii. Capacity building for government personnel C. Aadhaar-enabled Payment System (AePS) iii. Aadhaar-based cashless banking D. PRAGATI iv. Financial inclusion through doorstep banking
questions.explanation: A. Dak Karmayogi → ii. Capacity building for government personnel Dak Karmayogi is an initiative of the Department of Posts aimed at training and skill development of postal employees to improve service quality and efficiency. B. India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) → iv. Financial inclusion through doorstep banking India Post Payments Bank was launched to promote financial inclusion, especially in rural areas, by providing banking services at customers’ doorstep through post offices and postal staff. C. Aadhaar-enabled Payment System (AePS) → iii. Aadhaar-based cashless banking AePS allows banking transactions using Aadhaar authentication, enabling basic banking services like withdrawal and balance enquiry without debit cards. D. PRAGATI → i. Infrastructure monitoring PRAGATI (Pro-Active Governance and Timely Implementation) is a platform for monitoring major government projects and infrastructure development at the highest level

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Parts of the Constitution of India: • Part VIII of the Constitution deals with the administration of Union Territories. • Part IX of the Constitution relates to Panchayats and was added by the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992. • Part IX-A deals with Co-operative Societies and was inserted by the 97th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2011. • Part IX-B deals with Municipalities. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 - Correct Part VIII deals with Union Territories (Articles 239–242). Statement 2 - Correct Part IX deals with Panchayats. It was added by the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992. Statement 3 - Incorrect Part IX-B (not IX-A) deals with Co-operative Societies. It was inserted by the 97th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2011. Statement 4 - Incorrect Part IX-A deals with Municipalities, added by the 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992.

questions.question: Match the geological feature with its primary plate boundary type: • Mid-ocean ridge • Mariana Trench • San Andreas Fault
questions.explanation: 1. Mid-Ocean Ridge → Divergent Plate Boundary At divergent boundaries, two tectonic plates move away from each other. Magma rises from the mantle to fill the gap and forms new oceanic crust. This continuous creation of crust forms long underwater mountain chains called mid-ocean ridges (e.g., the Mid-Atlantic Ridge). Therefore, Mid-ocean ridge = Divergent boundary. 2. Mariana Trench → Convergent Plate Boundary At convergent boundaries, two plates move toward each other. One plate (usually denser oceanic plate) subducts beneath the other into the mantle. This subduction creates very deep ocean trenches. The Mariana Trench in the Pacific Ocean is the deepest part of the Earth’s oceans, formed by subduction of the Pacific Plate. Therefore, Mariana Trench = Convergent boundary. 3. San Andreas Fault → Transform Plate Boundary At transform boundaries, tectonic plates slide horizontally past each other. No crust is created or destroyed, but strong earthquakes occur due to friction. The San Andreas Fault in California is a famous example where the Pacific Plate and North American Plate slide past each other. Therefore, San Andreas Fault = Transform boundary.

questions.question: What is the name of India’s first AI teacher introduced in 2024?
questions.explanation: In March 2024, a school in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala introduced ‘Iris’, which has been widely reported as India’s first AI-enabled teacher robot. It was developed in collaboration with Makerlabs Edutech and unveiled at KTCT Higher Secondary School to enhance classroom learning using generative AI technology.

questions.question: During the early phase of the Indian national movement, some leaders strongly criticised the Moderates for following the “politics of prayer, petition and protest.” Which of the following leaders were associated with this criticism? • Bipin Chandra Pal • Bal Gangadhar Tilak • Lala Lajpat Rai • Gopal Krishna Gokhale
questions.explanation: In the early phase of the freedom struggle, leaders of the Indian National Congress were divided into two groups: • Moderates – believed in petitions, prayers, and constitutional methods to request reforms from the British government. • Extremists – believed that these methods were too weak and demanded strong political action and self-rule. Leaders like Bal Gangadhar Tilak, Bipin Chandra Pal, and Lala Lajpat Rai strongly criticised the Moderates’ approach and called it “politics of prayer, petition and protest.” These three leaders are famously known as the Lal-Bal-Pal trio, representing the Extremist wing of the national movement.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding certain Indian festivals and cultural traditions: • Navroz, celebrated by the Parsi community, marks the Persian New Year and is based on the spring equinox. • The Tulip Festival is organised in the Indira Gandhi Memorial Tulip Garden located in Srinagar. • Raas Leela performances in Mathura during Holi primarily depict episodes from the Ramayana. • Navroz has been recognised by UNESCO as part of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 - Correct Navroz marks the Persian New Year and is celebrated on the spring equinox (around 21 March) by Parsis and other Zoroastrian communities. Statement 2 - Correct The Tulip Festival is held at the Indira Gandhi Memorial Tulip Garden in Srinagar, Asia’s largest tulip garden. Statement 3 - Incorrect Raas Leela performances depict episodes from the life of Lord Krishna (Bhagavata Purana), not the Ramayana. Statement 4 - Correct Nowruz/Navroz has been inscribed by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.

questions.question: What were the coins minted in Delhi during the reign of Tomar and Chauhan called?
questions.explanation: During the rule of the Tomar dynasty and later the Chauhan dynasty in Delhi, a special type of coin was issued called Dehliwal (Dehliwala). Key points: • Dehliwal coins were mainly copper coins. • They were circulated in Delhi and nearby regions before the establishment of the Delhi Sultanate. • These coins were commonly used in local trade and markets. • Later rulers of the Delhi Sultanate introduced coins like Tanka and Jital

questions.question: Which of the following pairs (Governor-General/Statesman – Policy/Action) is correctly matched?
options: Lord Hastings – Advocated strict non-intervention in Indian princely states
Marquis of Hastings – Pursued a policy of aggressive expansion against the Marathas and Gurkhas
Lord Wellesley – Opposed the Subsidiary Alliance system
Henry Dundas – Actively campaigned against the renewal of the East India Company’s Charter in 1813
questions.explanation: Option A - incorrect Francis Rawdon-Hastings (Marquis of Hastings) did not follow non-intervention. He actually abandoned earlier restraint and fought: The Third Anglo-Maratha War (1817–18) The Anglo-Nepal War (1814–16) So, calling him anti-expansionist is incorrect. Option B - correct The Marquis of Hastings followed an expansionist policy, consolidating British supremacy in India after defeating the Marathas and Gurkhas. This is correctly matched. Option C - incorrect Richard Wellesley was the architect of the Subsidiary Alliance System and pursued imperialist expansion. He did not oppose it — he introduced and expanded it. Option D - incorrect Henry Dundas was associated with control over Company affairs but did not oppose the Charter renewal in that manner.

questions.question: Which law replaced the Indian Penal Code (IPC), 1860?
questions.explanation: The Indian Penal Code has been replaced by the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita. The new criminal law reforms introduced in 2023 replaced three major colonial-era laws: • IPC, 1860 → Replaced by Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023 • CrPC, 1973 → Replaced by Bharatiya Nagrik Suraksha Sanhita • Indian Evidence Act, 1872 → Replaced by Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam

questions.question: Which of the following statements best distinguishes the Elephanta Caves from many other early Indian rock-cut monuments?
options: They are predominantly Buddhist excavations centred around large chaitya halls and stupas.
They represent mature Shaivite iconography, with high-relief sculptures designed to create dramatic effects of light and shadow within the mandapa.
They combine Jain Tirthankara images with Vaishnavite themes inside pillared monasteries.
They are monolithic free-standing structures commissioned during the Mauryan period.
questions.explanation: Option A - wrong This describes early Buddhist cave sites like Ajanta or Karla, where chaitya halls and stupas dominate. Elephanta is not primarily Buddhist. Option B - Right Elephanta Caves are famous for their Shaivite themes, especially the grand Trimurti (three-faced Shiva) sculpture. The caves date roughly to the 6th century CE. The sculptures are carved in high relief, meaning figures project strongly from the rock surface. The interior layout allows natural light to fall on sculptures, enhancing depth and dramatic shadow effects — a distinctive artistic feature. This sculptural grandeur and theological focus on Shiva set Elephanta apart. Option C - wrong Elephanta does not primarily feature Jain or Vaishnavite imagery. Its dominant theme is Shaivism. Option D - wrong Elephanta Caves are rock-cut excavations, not free-standing monolithic structures like later temples (e.g., Kailasa at Ellora). Also, they are not from the Mauryan period (Mauryan caves like Barabar are much earlier and simpler).

questions.question: Read the following statements carefully and choose the correct option regarding Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Assertion (A): The disinvestment policy in India has faced sustained criticism from various sections of society. Reason (R): Disinvestment has often led to concerns about undervaluation of public assets and possible concentration of economic power in private hands.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) - True India’s disinvestment policy (sale of government stake in PSUs) has been criticized on grounds such as: Loss of public sector control Risk to employment Sale of strategic assets Reason (R) - True Critics argue that: Public assets are sometimes sold at prices considered low (undervaluation). Strategic disinvestment may increase private sector concentration of economic power, reducing competition in certain sectors. This directly explains why the policy has been criticized

questions.question: Express the recurring decimal 0.4363636... in the form p/q, where p and q are integers and q ≠ 0. What is the value of p/q in its simplest form?
questions.explanation: Let x be the decimal: x = 0.4363636 …… (1) The repeating part is 36. 10x = 4.363636 …… (2) 1000x = 436.3636364 …… (3) Now, equation (3) – equation (2): 1000x – 10x = 436.363636... - 4.363636... 990x = 432 x = 432 / 990 = 24 / 55 Hence, p/q in its simplest form is 24/55.

questions.question: The vertices of a triangle are given by the coordinates P(0, 0), Q(12, 0), and R(0, 12). Find the coordinates of the circumcenter of this triangle.
questions.explanation: Since the line PQ lies on the x-axis and the line PR lies on the y-axis, the angle at vertex P(0, 0) is a right angle (90°). Therefore, triangle PQR is a right-angled triangle. In any right-angled triangle, the circumcenter is always located at the midpoint of the hypotenuse. Here, segment QR is the hypotenuse. Using mid-point formula: Coordinates of the Circumcenter = [(12+0)/2, (0+12)/2] = (6, 6)

questions.question: A drone directly above point X is rising vertically at a constant speed of 5 m/s. An observer at point Y, which is 600 m due west of X, is watching the drone. At a certain moment, the angle of elevation from the observer to the drone is 30°. How many seconds (apprx.) will it take for the drone to reach an angle of elevation of 60° (assuming there’s no horizontal drift)?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1775550275Math%20Paid%20-%2017_html_1a86ae1f.gif) In ∆AXY, tan 30° = AX / XY 1 / √3 = AX / 600 AX = 200√3 m Now, In ∆BXY, tan 60° = BX / XY √3 = BX / 600 BX = 600√3 m As we know, Time = distance / speed The distance travelled by the drone = BX – AX = 400√3 m and speed of the drone = 5 m/s. Thus, Time = 400√3 / 5 = 80√3 seconds = 80 × 1.732 = 138.56 seconds

questions.question: A container holds 160 liters of a milk and water solution, where 25% of the mixture is milk. If 40 liters of this mixture is removed and replaced with another solution that contains 45% milk, what is the final percentage of milk in the resulting mixture?
questions.explanation: Total mixture = 160 liters Initial qty. of milk = 25% of 160 = 40 liters When 40 liters of the mixture is removed, it contains the same 25% concentration of milk. Milk removed = 25% of 40 = 0.25 × 40 = 10 liters Remaining milk in 120 liters mixture = 40 – 10 = 30 liters Now, Volume of new solution added = 40 liters, which contains 45% milk. Thus, Milk added = 45% of 40 = 18 liters Now, Total milk in the new mixture = 30 + 18 = 48 liters Total volume = (120+40) liters = 160 liters Final milk percentage = (Total milk / Total volume) × 100 = (48 / 160) × 100 = 30%

questions.question: The ratio of Silver to Zinc in an alloy is 3 : 5. If the price of Zinc is 1/8th the price of Silver, find the total cost of the alloy if the cost of the Silver in the alloy is 48,000 rupees.
questions.explanation: Silver Weight : Zinc Weight = 3 : 5 Silver Price : Zinc Price = 8 : 1 Ratio of Silver Cost to Zinc Cost: (3 × 8) : (5 × 1) = 24 : 5 Now, ATQ: 24 parts ⇨ Rs. 48,000 1 part  Rs. 48,000 / 24 = Rs. 2,000 Total cost is the sum of both parts: 29 parts  29 × Rs. 2,000 = Rs. 58,000.

questions.question: Pipe X and Y together can fill a tank in 12 minutes. Pipe Y and Z together can fill the same tank in 15 minutes. Pipe Z and X together can fill the same tank in 20 minutes. Firstly, pipe Z is opened. After 'K' minutes, pipes X and Y are also opened. The remaining part of the tank gets filled in 5 minutes. What is the value of K?
questions.explanation: Let the total capacity of the tank be W. W = LCM of (12, 15, 20) = 60 units As we know, Efficiency = work(capacity) / time Efficiency of: X + Y = 60 / 12 = 5 units/min Y + Z = 60 / 15 = 4 units/min Z + X = 60 / 20 = 3 units/min On solving above three equations, we get: X = 2 units/min Y = 3 units/min Z = 1 unit/min ATQ: In the last 5 minutes, all three pipes were open. Work done in last 5 minutes = (Efficiency of X + Y + Z) × 5 minutes = 6 × 5 = 30 units The remaining work was done by Z before X and Y were opened. Remaining Work = Total Capacity – Work done in last 5 mins = 60 – 30 = 30 units. Thus, work done by Z alone before last 5 mins = 30 units. Since Z fills 1 unit per minute: K = Work done by Z alone / Efficiency of Z K = 30 units / 1 unit/min = 30 minutes.

questions.question: The annual profit percentage earned by a retail company from 2021-2024 is given below. Profit percentage is calculated by the difference between income and expenditure over expenditure. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1775550275Math%20Paid%20-%2017_html_475f2669.png) If the income in 2023 was 450 crore rupees, what was the expenditure in that same year?
questions.explanation: Profit %=[(Income-Expenditure)/Expenditure]×100 → Income=Expenditure×{1+(Profit %/100)} Income in 2023 = 450 crore rupees. Profit % in 2023 = 50%. Thus, 450=Expenditure×{1+(50/100)} 450=Expenditure×1.5 Expenditure =450/1.5 = Rs. 300 crores

questions.question: Two friends, X and Y, invest Rs. 80,000 and Rs. 1,20,000 respectively in a small startup. After one year, the total profit is distributed such that each partner first receives 8% per annum simple interest on their initial investment, and the remaining profit is divided in the ratio of their investments. If the total profit including interest is Rs. 46,000, what is the total share of X?
questions.explanation: Both receive 8% simple interest on their initial capital for one year. Interest received by: X = 8% of Rs. 80,000 = Rs. 6,400 Y = 8% of Rs. 1,20,000 = Rs. 9,600 Total Interest Distributed = Rs. 6,400 + Rs. 9,600 = Rs. 16,000 Remaining Profit = Total Profit – Total Interest = 46,000 – 16,000 = Rs. 30,000 The remaining profit is divided in the ratio of their investments. Now, investment ratio (X : Y) = 80,000 : 1,20,000 = 2 : 3 Thus, X's share from remaining profit = (2/5) × Rs. 30,000 = Rs. 12,000 Hence, Total Share of X = Interest + Profit Share = Rs. 6,400 + Rs. 12,000 = Rs. 18,400

questions.question: The average of 30 numbers is 64. If 40% of the numbers are increased by 8 each and the remaining 60% of the numbers are decreased by 5 each, what is the new average of the numbers?
questions.explanation: Initial sum = Average × Total numbers = 64 × 30 = 1920 ATQ: 40% of the numbers are increased by 8 each. 40% of the numbers = 40% of 30 = (40 / 100) × 30 = 12 numbers. Thus, total increase = 12 × 8 = 96 And, The remaining numbers are 60%. 60% of the numbers = 60% of 30 = (60 / 100) × 30 = 18 numbers. Total decrease = 18 × 5 = 90 Net change = Total increase – Total decrease = 96 – 90 = +6 Thus, New sum = Initial sum + Net change = 1920 + 6 = 1926 New average = New sum / Total numbers = 1926 / 30 = 64.2

questions.question: The ratio of the simple interest received when (x + 1000) rupees is invested at 15% per annum for 4 years to the compound interest received when (x - 1000) rupees is invested at 10% per annum (compounded annually) for 2 years is 10 : 1. Find the value of x.
A trapezoid is circumscribed around a circle. The lengths of the two non-parallel sides are 9 cm and 12 cm. Determine the total perimeter of the trapezoid.
questions.explanation: When a quadrilateral is circumscribed around a circle, the sum of the lengths of one pair of opposite sides is equal to that of the other pair of opposite sides. If the trapezoid has sides a, b, c, and d, where a and b are the parallel sides (bases) and c and d are the non-parallel sides (legs), the property states: a + b = c + d →a + b = 9 + 12 →a + b = 21 cm The perimeter (P) of the trapezoid is the sum of all four sides: P = (a + b) + (c + d) P = 21 + 21 = 42 cm

questions.question: An L-shaped garden consists of two rectangular plots. The first plot measures 5 m by 3 m, and the second plot measures 10 m by 6 m. The cost of laying premium grass is 550 rupees per square meter. However, the contractor offers a 12% discount if the total area exceeds 65 square meters. What is the total cost of laying the grass after the discount?
questions.explanation: Total area of the garden = sum of areas of both plots = (5×3) m2 + (10×6) m2 = 75 m2 Since, total Area (75 m2) is greater than 65 m2, therefore, the 12% discount is applicable. Thus, Total cost = 88% of Rs. [550 × 75] = Rs. 0.88 × 550 × 75 = Rs. 36,300

questions.question: The volume of a right circular cone is 60% of the volume of a right circular cylinder. The height of the cylinder is 20% more than the height of the cone. If the radius of the cone is 24 cm, find the radius of the cylinder.
questions.explanation: Let, Rc (Radius of Cone) = 24 cm Hc (Height of Cone) Vc (Volume of Cone) Rz (Radius of Cylinder) Hz (Height of Cylinder) Vz (Volume of Cylinder) ATQ: Hz = 120% of Hc = 1.2 × Hc Vc = 60% of Vz = 0.6 × Vz Now, as we know, Vc = (1/3) × π × (Rc2) × H­c And V­z = π × (Rz2) × Hz Now, ATQ: (1/3) × π × (Rc2) × H­c = 0.6 × [π × (Rz2) × Hz] → (1/3) × (Rc2) × H­c = 0.6 × (Rz2) × 1.2H­c → (1/3) × (24 × 24) = 0.72 × (Rz2) → (1/3) × 576 = 0.72 × (Rz2) →192 = 0.72 × (Rz2) → Rz2 = 192 / 0.72 = 800 / 3 Rz = √(800/3) = 20√(2/3) cm

questions.question: 40 workers working 9 hours a day can complete 6 units of a project in 15 days. How many additional numbers of workers would be required to complete 10 units of the same project in 12 days if they work for 10 hours a day?
questions.explanation: The amount of work done is directly proportional to the number of workers, the days worked, and the hours per day, i.e., (M1 × D1 × H1) / W1 = (M2 × D2 × H2) / W2 Where: M = Number of workers D = Number of days H = Hours per day W = Amount of work Now, (40 × 15 × 9) / 6 = (M2 × 12 × 10) / 10 M2 = 75 Additional workers = Total needed – Initial workers = 75 – 40 = 35

questions.question: A committee of 6 members is to be formed from a group of 8 men and 7 women. If the committee is selected at random, what is the probability that the committee contains at least 4 women?
questions.explanation: Total number of people = 8 men + 7 women = 15 people. We need to select 6 members out of 15. Total ways of selection = 15C6 ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1775550275Math%20Paid%20-%2017_html_b1633ddd.png) Let’s identify Favorable Cases (At least 4 women): i.e., we can have 4, 5, or 6 women in the committee. Case 1: 4 Women and 2 Men in the committee– Ways of selection = (7C4) × (8C2) = 35 × 28 = 980 Case 2: 5 Women and 1 Man in the committee– Ways of selection = (7C5) × (8C1) = 21 × 8 = 168 Case 3: 6 Women and 0 Men in the committee– Ways of selection = (7C6) × (8C0) = 7 × 1 = 7 Total no. of favorable outcomes = 980 + 168 + 7 = 1155 As we know, Probability = Total Favorable Ways / Total Ways = 1155 / 5005 = 3 / 13

questions.question: If sin (4x - 10°) is equal to cos (x + 20°), and both are acute angles, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: For any two acute angles A and B, if sin (A) = cos (B), then they are complementary, i.e. A + B = 90° Thus, (4x - 10°) + (x + 20°) = 90° 5x = 80° x = 16

questions.question: Identify the incorrect relationship(s) from the list below: i. √8 + √2 = √7 + √3 ii. √8 + √2 > √7 + √3 iii. √8 + √2

Two runners, X and Y, start from the same point on a circular track of 600 meters. They run in the same direction with speeds of 12 m/s and 8 m/s, respectively. How many times will they cross each other in 20 minutes?
questions.explanation: Total time = 20 × 60 = 1200 seconds Since both runners are running in the same direction, Relative Speed = 12 m/s – 8 m/s = 4 m/s Now, Time for one meeting = Track Length / Relative Speed = 600 / 4 = 150 seconds Thus, Total number of meetings = Total Time / Time for one meeting = 1200 / 150 = 8

questions.question: Consider an isosceles right triangle ABC where the angle at vertex C measures 90° and M is the midpoint of hypotenuse AB. What can be said about point M?
options: M is the incenter of the triangle.
M is the centroid of the triangle.
M is the circumcenter of the triangle.
Nothing can be deduced about M using the given information.
questions.explanation: In any right-angled triangle, the midpoint of the hypotenuse is equidistant from all three vertices. i.e., AM = BM = CM If you were to draw a circle passing through points A, B, and C, M would be the center of that circle. Hence, M is the circumcenter of triangle ABC.

questions.question: If (a + b) : (b + c) : (c + a) = 8 : 11 : 15 for positive reals a, b, c, then find the ratio b : c : a.
questions.explanation: Let the common multiplier for the ratios be k. Thus, a + b = 8k ……(1) b + c = 11k ……(2) c + a = 15k ……(3) On solving equations (1), (2) and (3), we get: c = 9k a = 6k b = 2k Thus, b : c : a = 2 : 9 : 6

questions.question: In a 2 km race, Arjun and Bhishm run together, and Arjun wins by 40 seconds. Arjun and Karan also run the same race, and Arjun beats Karan by 800 meters. Later, when Bhishm and Karan run the same distance, Bhishm wins by 20 seconds. How much time (in seconds) did Arjun take to cover the 2 km?
questions.explanation: Time for each runner: Let for Arjun be: T seconds Bhishm: T + 40 seconds (Arjun wins by 40s) Karan: T + 60 seconds (Bhishm wins by 20s) Arjun beats Karan by 800m in a 2000 m race. →When Arjun finishes 2000 m race in T seconds, Karan has only covered: 2000 m – 800 m = 1200 m Karan's speed = 1200 / T m/s Karan covers the full 2000 m in (T + 60) seconds. Time = Distance / Speed T + 60 = 2000 / (1200 / T) T + 60 = (5 / 3) × T 3 × (T + 60) = 5T 2T = 180 T = 90 seconds

questions.question: A solid right cone with a base radius of 6 cm is completely submerged in a rectangular tank with a base measuring 20 cm by 11 cm. The tank was initially filled with water to a certain level. After the cone is submerged, the water level in the tank rises by 3 cm. Find the perpendicular height of the cone.
questions.explanation: The water level in the rectangular tank rises by 3 cm. This rise in volume forms a rectangular prism with the base of the tank. Base of the tank = 20 cm ×11 cm Rise in height = 3 cm Volume of displaced water = Length × Width × Rise = 20 × 11 × 3 = 660 cm3 Now, Since the cone is completely submerged, its volume is equal to the volume of the displaced water. Let the base radius of the cone be r = 6 cm, perpendicular height of the cone be h cm. Volume of Cone = 660 cm3 (1/3) × π × (r2) × h = 660 (1/3) × (22/7) × (6 × 6) × h = 660 (22/7) × 12 × h = 660 h = (660 × 7) / 264 h = 4620 / 264 h = 17.5 cm Hence, perpendicular height of the cone is 17.5 cm.

questions.question: A retailer buys three varieties of tea: Grade A, Grade B, and Grade C. The ratio of their selling prices is 12 : 25 : 18. He makes a profit of 20% on Grade A and 25% on Grade B, but he incurs a loss of 10% on Grade C. What is his approximate overall percentage gain or loss in the entire transaction?
questions.explanation: The ratio of SP is 12 : 25 : 18. To make the percentage calculations easy, let's assume the actual selling prices in rupees: SP of Grade A = Rs. 120 SP of Grade B = Rs. 250 SP of Grade C = Rs. 180 Total SP of all three grades = Rs. (120 + 250 + 180) = Rs. 550 Now, CP = SP / (1 + Profit %) And, CP = SP / (1 – Loss %) CP of Grade A (20% Profit): CPA = 120 / 1.20 = Rs. 100 CP of Grade B (25% Profit): CPB = 250 / 1.25 = Rs. 200 CP of Grade C (10% Loss): CPC = 180 / 0.90 = Rs. 200 Total CP of all three grades = Rs. (100 + 200 + 200) = Rs. 500 Total Gain = Rs. 550 - Rs. 500 = Rs. 50 Gain % = (Total Gain / Total CP) × 100% = (50 / 500) × 100% = 10%

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: The technical specifications of the new
software are so intricate to be understood
by a layman without prior coding experience.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). Replace "so" with "too". 📌 Error concept: This is an error based on the incorrect use of adverbial pairs. In English grammar, the structure "too + adjective + to + V1" is used to convey a negative meaning (meaning something is "more than enough" to the point that a certain result cannot happen). The word "so" is typically paired with "that" to form a clause, not with a "to-infinitive." Grammatical concept: To express that a quality is so extreme that it prevents an action, we use the Too...to structure. Structure 1 (Negative Result): Subject + Verb + too + Adjective + to + V1. Example: He is too weak to walk. (Meaning: He is so weak that he cannot walk). Structure 2 (So...that): Subject + Verb + so + Adjective + that + Clause. Example: The specifications are so intricate that they cannot be understood. In the given sentence, the presence of "to be understood" (to + V1) acts as a structural hint that the preceding adverb must be "too" rather than "so."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The con artist tried to ___________ the counterfeit diamond as a genuine heirloom to the unsuspecting buyer.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - The correct phrasal verb is "pass off," which means to represent something as being different from what it actually is, or to falsely convince someone that an item is genuine. In this context, the con artist is trying to make a fake diamond appear real. Detailed Analysis of Options: Pass off - किसी चीज़ को कुछ और बताकर धोखा देना / असली बताकर नकली चीज़ बेचना - To falsely represent something or someone as being different from what they really are Example: He tried to pass off the costume jewelry as real gold. Pass away - मृत्यु हो जाना / निधन होना - To die (used as a polite expression). Example: After a long illness, the old man passed away last night. Pass out - बेहोश हो जाना - To become unconscious Example: The marathon runner passed out due to extreme dehydration. Pass over - नजरअंदाज करना / ध्यान न देना - To ignore or disregard someone or something; to fail to promote someone. Example: Though he was the most experienced candidate, he was passed over for the promotion.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The arrogant billionaire used to sneer on the humble lifestyle of his childhood friends.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) 📌 Explanation - The underlined part "sneer on" is grammatically incorrect because the verb sneer always takes the fixed preposition 'at' when it refers to the person or thing being mocked. Grammatical Concept: The word 'Sneer' (meaning to smile or speak in a contemptuous or mocking manner) is part of a specific group of verbs that express facial expressions or reactions directed at someone/something. These verbs almost always take the preposition 'at'. Key Examples of "Verb + At" for Mockery/Reaction: Sneer/scoff at (मुस्कुराकर उपहास उड़ाना) Laugh at (हँसना/मज़ाक उड़ाना) Mock at (मज़ाक उड़ाना) Jeer at (चिढ़ाना) Smile at (मुस्कुराना) Gaze at (घूरना/एकटक देखना)

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: Neither the scout leader nor the scouts was prepared for the sudden storm.
It is I who is responsible for the delay in the project completion.
The number of candidates appearing for the exam have increased this year.
Many a man has realized the importance of health after falling ill.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) 📌 Explanation - This sentence is grammatically correct. The structure "Many a" is followed by a singular noun ("man") and a singular verb ("has"). It is a formal way of expressing that many individuals have had the same experience. Detailed Analysis of Other wrong Options: Option A: Error: The verb should be "were" instead of "was." When subjects are joined by "neither... nor," the verb must agree with the nearest subject (the scouts), which is plural. Option B: Error: It should be "It is I who am." When a relative pronoun (who) is used, the verb must agree with its antecedent. Here, the antecedent is "I," which takes the verb "am." Option C: Error: The verb should be "has" instead of "have." While the phrase "A number of" takes a plural verb, the phrase "The number of" always takes a singular verb because it refers to the specific total or unit.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. I feel frustrated that she keeps reverting back to her bad habits after brief phases of positive change.
options: keeps reverting to her bad habits
keeps reverting backward to her bad habits
keeps reverting forth to her bad habits
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A) - keeps reverting to her bad habits. 📌 Explanation - The original sentence contains a redundancy error. The word "revert" itself means "to go back" or "to return to a previous state." Adding the word "back" after it creates a "pleonasm" (using more words than necessary), which is grammatically incorrect in formal English. Therefore, "back" must be removed. Why other options are wrong: 1) Option (b): "Reverting backward" is redundant for the same reason "back" is. 2) Option (c): "Reverting forth" is contextually illogical as revert implies a backward movement, not a forward one.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. Despite the team's initial commitment to a streamlined workflow, their communication protocols began to __________ into a chaotic mess of overlapping emails and missed deadlines.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D)- devolve. 📌 Explanation - The sentence describes a process of deterioration, where a "streamlined workflow" transitions into a "chaotic mess." The word devolve is the most appropriate fit as it means to transfer or degenerate into a lower or worse state. It perfectly captures the downward spiral of the team's communication. Explanation for close options (2 & 4)- While both words (devolve and retrograde) imply a "backward" or "downward" movement, Devolve is specifically used for a functional or moral decline (from order to chaos). Retrograde usually describes a physical direction or a specific step/policy that goes against progress. Since the sentence talks about a "workflow" turning into a "mess," devolve is the standard idiomatic choice. Meanings and Explanations of Options: Recidivate - अपराध या बुरी आदत में फिर से पड़ना - To fall back or lapse into a previous undesirable behavior or crime. Example: Many criminals recidivate within a year of their release due to a lack of employment. Retrograde - प्रतिगामी / पीछे की ओर जाने वाला - Directed or moving backward; reverting to an inferior or less developed state. Example: The company's decision to cut employee benefits was seen as a retrograde step by the union. Egress - निकास / बाहर निकलने का मार्ग - The action of going out of or leaving a place; an exit. Example: The heavy curtains blocked the emergency egress, creating a major safety hazard. Devolve - बदतर स्थिति में चले जाना / अवनति होना - To degenerate or pass into a lower or worse state. Example: The peaceful protest began to devolve into a violent riot as the night went on.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: In the current fiscal year, the renewable energy sector
has shown a more consistent growth trajectory
than any industry in the domestic market.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (C). 👉 Replace "any" with "any other". 📌 Error concept: This is an error of Exclusion in Comparison. When comparing a specific entity (renewable energy sector) to the category it belongs to (industries in the domestic market), the subject must be excluded from its own group using "other." Grammatical Concept: In English, when using the Comparative Degree (more consistent... than) to compare one member of a group with the rest of the same group, you must use "any other" or "all other." Without "other," the sentence implies that the renewable energy sector is more consistent than itself, which is a logical fallacy. Important Distinction (The "Different Group" Exception): If the subject is NOT part of the group it is being compared to, do not use "other." In our question: Since the renewable energy sector is an industry in the domestic market, "other" is mandatory.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The engineering firm submitted a detailed project proposal to the city council for the new bridge.
options: The architect spent weeks drafting the blueprints for the upcoming skyscraper.
Scientists predict that the new telescope will project clear images of distant galaxies.
The team leader assigned specific tasks to each member of the research group.
It is vital to manage your timeline carefully when working on a long-term assignment.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) 👉 Explanation - This is a "Homonym" question. Homonyms are words that have the same spelling and often the same pronunciation but different meanings. In the Question: "Project" is used as a Noun which means a planned piece of work or a specific task (परियोजना). In Option B: "Project" is used as a Verb which means to cause an image, light, or shadow to fall upon a surface (प्रक्षेपित करना). This is a different grammatical category and meaning, making it the correct homonym choice. WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG: Options A, C, and D are wrong because they use words like "drafting," "tasks," and "assignment." While these words relate to the meaning of a "planned piece of work" (the noun form of project), they are synonyms, not homonyms. To be a homonym, the sentence must use the word "project" but in a different sense. Since these options don't use the word "project" at all, they cannot be the correct answer.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. Once the preliminary validation is complete, analysts deploy automated scripts to cross-reference the submitted datasets against historical benchmarks to detect statistical anomalies. B. This rigorous multi-stage verification process ensures that the final reports generated are not only accurate but also resistant to the biases inherent in raw, unvetted data. C. The initial phase of any robust data architecture begins with the systematic collection of user submissions, which are immediately filtered through a syntax-check layer to ensure structural integrity. D. Following this, any discrepancies identified during the cross-referencing stage are flagged for manual review, where subject matter experts determine if the outliers represent genuine shifts or simple entry errors.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct option is (B) - CADB 📌 Explanation - To solve this logically, we look for "opening indicators" and "transitional keywords" - 1) Opening Sentence: Sentence (C) is the clear opener because it introduces the "initial phase" and the start of the "data architecture." It sets the stage by talking about the collection and first filtering of data. 2) Establishing the Sequence: * After the "initial phase" in (C), sentence (A) follows naturally with "Once the preliminary validation is complete." This refers back to the syntax-check mentioned in (C). Sentence (D) starts with "Following this" and mentions "discrepancies identified during the cross-referencing stage." This directly links to the automated "cross-reference" mentioned in sentence (A). 3)The Conclusion: Sentence (B) acts as the summary. It uses the demonstrative phrase "This rigorous multi-stage verification process" to refer to the entire workflow described in C, A, and D, making it the perfect closing statement. Final Paragraph: The initial phase of any robust data architecture begins with the systematic collection of user submissions, which are immediately filtered through a syntax-check layer to ensure structural integrity. Once the preliminary validation is complete, analysts deploy automated scripts to cross-reference the submitted datasets against historical benchmarks to detect statistical anomalies. Following this, any discrepancies identified during the cross-referencing stage are flagged for manual review, where subject matter experts determine if the outliers represent genuine shifts or simple entry errors. This rigorous multi-stage verification process ensures that the final reports generated are not only accurate but also resistant to the biases inherent in raw, unvetted data.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The research committee has meticulously scrutinized every technical parameter of the experimental design.
options: Every technical parameter of the experimental design was meticulously scrutinized by the research committee.
Every technical parameter of the experimental design is being meticulously scrutinized by the research committee.
Every technical parameter of the experimental design had been meticulously scrutinized by the research committee.
Every technical parameter of the experimental design has been meticulously scrutinized by the research committee.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - The original sentence is in the Present Perfect Tense (has + scrutinized). When converting from Active to Passive voice, we must maintain the same tense. The structure for Present Perfect Passive is: Object + has/have + been + Verb (3rd form) + by + Subject.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The Professor said to the students, "Why did you not submit the research assignment before the deadline?"
options: The Professor asked the students why had they not submitted the research assignment before the deadline.
The Professor questioned the students why they have not submitted the research assignment before the deadline.
The Professor asked the students why they did not submit the research assignment before the deadline.
The Professor asked the students why they had not submitted the research assignment before the deadline.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) 📌 Explanation - When converting an interrogative sentence from Direct to Indirect speech, two major changes occur: Tense Change: The Simple Past (did not submit) changes into the Past Perfect (had not submitted). Sentence Structure: The interrogative form (Verb + Subject) changes into the Assertive form (Subject + Verb). Therefore, "Why did you..." becomes "Why they had..." Grammatical Concept: Interrogative Narration Rules Reporting Verb: "Said to" changes to asked, questioned, or enquired. Connective: For "Wh-" questions (Why, Where, When, etc.), no conjunction (like that or if) is used. The "Wh-" word itself acts as the connector. Tense Shift: Simple Past ➡️ Past Perfect (V2 / {did} ➡️ {had} + V3)

questions.question: The challenge in Madras high court to the Emergency-era shifting of education from state list to concurrent list ___(1)___ a futile exercise in turning back the clock. Forty-six years have passed ___(2)___ the 42 Constitution Amendment Act. In this period, the education sector has seen far too many changes, and most can’t be ___(3)___ . For instance, a common national medical test now allows students the ease of appearing for just one examination instead of multiple ones conducted by each state. States still enjoy ____(4)___ to operate their own universities, schools and education syllabus. Private sector is also flourishing in education. And the policy tinkering at all levels goes on. UGC is restructuring UG, PG and doctoral programmes. States are trying out clustering of schools, closing down smaller schools. Instruction in English seems to have lost favour at the Centre, even though society is ___(5)____ headed in the opposite direction. But the moot point is no one entity can claim complete control. It is a diverse ecosystem. Far more important issues than locating education in the Constitution are at stake. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d)- is. 💢 Explanation - The subject of the sentence is "The challenge" (singular), referring to the legal petition in the Madras High Court. Even though the sentence contains plural phrases like "State list" or "Concurrent list," the verb must agree with the main singular head-word. Since the passage discusses a present, ongoing viewpoint regarding this legal action, the singular present tense "is" is required. Why others are wrong: (a) had: This is past tense; using it implies the challenge used to be futile but isn't anymore, which contradicts the author's current stance. (b) were: This is plural and cannot be used with the singular subject "The challenge." (c) have: This is a plural verb and grammatically incorrect here.

questions.question: The challenge in Madras high court to the Emergency-era shifting of education from state list to concurrent list ___(1)___ a futile exercise in turning back the clock. It is a diverse ecosystem. Far more important issues than locating education in the Constitution are at stake. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (b) - since. 💢 Explanation - The sentence uses the Present Perfect Tense ("Forty-six years have passed"). In English grammar, we use "since" to refer to a specific point in time in the past (the 1976 Amendment) from which a period of time continues to the present. Why others are wrong: (a) from: Usually used for a starting point in a "from... to..." construction, but it doesn't pair with the present perfect "have passed" in this context. (c) for: This is used for a duration (e.g., for 46 years), not to point back to a specific event like an Act. (d) as: This functions as a conjunction for reason or time (while), which does not fit a time-reference marker.

questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (a) – reversed. 💢 Explanation - The passage states that the education sector has seen too many changes to go back. In policy and legal contexts, when a change is undone or a process is turned back to its original state, it is said to be "reversed." Why others are wrong - (b) turned: To mean "undone," it would need to be "turned back." Alone, it is incomplete. (c) backed: This is not a standard term for undoing policy or historical changes. (d) returned: While it implies going back, "reversed" is the correct transitive verb for an action or policy that is officially undone.

questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d) flexibility 💢 Explanation - We need a noun here after the verb "enjoy." The sentence explains that while some things are centralized, states still have the freedom or latitude to run their own institutions. "Flexibility" is a noun that fits the grammatical requirement and the meaning of having options. Why others are wrong: (a) suitability: This means being "right or appropriate," which doesn't fit the context of having the power to operate schools. (b) suitable: This is an adjective; you cannot "enjoy suitable to operate." (c) flexible: This is also an adjective; the structure requires a noun.

questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (a) – irrevocably. 💢 Explanation - We need an adverb to describe the verb "headed." The passage suggests that society is moving toward English in a way that cannot be changed or taken back. "Irrevocably" means in a way that cannot be undone. Why others are wrong: b) irrelevant: This is an adjective meaning "not connected"; it doesn't make sense to be "irrelevant headed." (c) irrevocable: This is an adjective. We need an adverb (-ly) to modify the verb "headed." (d) suddenly: While grammatically possible, it doesn't match the context of a long-term societal shift as well as "irrevocably" does.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Demarcation Let’s see the meaning of all words: Epitome - आदर्श - a person or thing that is a perfect example Example: She's the epitome of elegance. Demarcation - सीमांकन - the act of marking boundaries Example: The demarcation of the land was clear. Conciliatory - समझौतापूर्ण - intended to show willingness to end a disagreement Example: He made a conciliatory gesture. Resplendence - शोभा - the quality of being splendid or magnificent Example: The resplendence of the palace was breathtaking.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – Waking up to the sound of waves crashing on the beach, she felt pure bliss and forgot all her worries.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Ecstasy. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Bliss - परमानंद - perfect happiness or joy Example: Spending time with her family gave her a sense of bliss. Booty -लूट का माल - treasure or loot Example: The pirate buried his booty on a deserted island. Forfeit - दंड - something given up or lost as a penalty Example: He had to forfeit his prize money due to doping charges. questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Frazzle

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- He struggled to disentangle himself from the awkward conversation.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Confuse Let’s see the meaning of all words: Disentangle - उलझन को सुलझाना - to free something or someone from a complicated situation Example: He struggled to disentangle himself from the awkward conversation. Herbage - वनस्पति - plants or vegetation Example: The botanist studied the local herbage for medicinal properties. Confuse - भ्रमित करना - to make something unclear or difficult to understand Example: The complicated instructions confused me. Atonement - प्रायश्चित - making amends or reparations for a wrong or injury Example: He made atonement for his mistake by apologizing.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. The company took a cue from its competitor and launched a new product line.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Extremely cold

questions.question: A nearby restaurant you often visit is regularly dumping wastewater containing food scraps directly into a public gutter near their back entrance. This practice can contaminate local water sources and cause blockages in the drainage system. What is the most effective way to handle this?
options: Stop visiting the restaurant and tell your family and friends to do the same.
Send an anonymous note to the restaurant owner warning them to stop.
Submit an official complaint to the local health or pollution control department.
Place a large sign by the gutter warning against pollution.
questions.explanation: Submitting an official complaint to the local health or pollution control department is the most effective action because these authorities have the power to investigate, enforce regulations, and impose penalties on polluters, ensuring long-term compliance. Informal methods like anonymous notes or signs may be ignored, while boycotting only affects business without addressing the environmental harm. Official channels, such as municipal health departments, handle such violations systematically, as seen in real cases where tips led to arrests for illegal grease dumping into drains.
